title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Execution Trace: Fanout Routing Contract
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must prove one queue per responsibility, late-subscriber behavior, cancellation, and independent consumer ownership; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fanout routing contract increment in a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map. Learners prog…
objectives: Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Fanout Routing Contract · Theory Fanout Routing Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 6636 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
